desc:
The two branches of the river that for many leagues have come down separately
from the mountains, finally merge at this spot into a single, mighty stream. As
the two flows rush into each other, there is a lot of turbulence in the troubled
waters, and eddies abound. The surface is no longer crystalline, but rather
cloudy, and one can hardly catch glimpse of the rock-strewn bottom. To the west,
the strengthened Bruinen continues its long way towards the sea.
name: Meeting of the Forks

desc:
The stream narrows in this area, confined between steep banks with slippery,
sharp stones. The rocks on both sides look scratched and full of fissures, with
pointy edges and deep crevices. These chinks are marks of river floods, when the
violent waters carry large boulders away, like dead leaves blown onwards in
gusts of wind. A bit further downstream, the flow roars noisily as both branches
of the river meet.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The trail moves its way through the edges of the forest, offering a peaceful
setting for your tired limbs. Maybe this would be a good place to rest, away
from the landscape of danger in the distances. The healthy green leaves offer a
balm to your eyes after the sights of past combat.
name: Border Trail

desc:
You stand on a trail just moving its way into an immense forest to the west. The
healthy green color of the trees raises your spirits. A small path leads off to
the north, into an area of spread out trees.
name: Border Trail

desc:
Standing here, you pause to admire the tall latticework of leaves and branches
that form a twinkling ceiling of lights above. A trail through the edge of the
forest is to the north, and more scattered trees lie to the south. The western
edge of the forest is quite thick, so thick as to prevent access.
name: Twinkling Canopy

desc:
This trail bridges various landscapes around you. To the south, old and decrepit
hills sweep onwards into the distance. A vast moor lies to the east, eventually
leading to the mighty Bruinen river. A forest of astounding height dominates the
western skies, filling you with awe at such natural majesty. To the north,
endless plains stretch beyond your sight. The choice is yours to which path will
suit you best.
name: Border Trail

desc:
This edge of the forest is quite lovely, with the tall branches of the oak and
pine trees rising high above you. Their leaves and needles block out most of the
light, but allow a sparkling display of colours to light the fallen leaves that
muffle your steps. Quickly though, the forest becomes a mass of thick trunks and
roots that block access to the west.
name: Twinkling Canopy

desc:
This slope is covered with tall and healthy trees, rising high above your head.
Their thick branches block most of the immediate light, bathing the area in
soothing shade. Jarring your senses, though, is the less healthy trees to your
immediate west, with greyish leaves and branches. The trees to the north are
more healthy looking, tempting you to forget your adventuresome instincts.
name: Forested Incline

desc:
The northern edge of these hills lies against the vast forest around you.
Greyish grass and brush covers the sides, in sharp contrast to the verdant green
of the treetops. However, you can't help but note that the two are somehow
connected, and that appearances are not always true indicators.
name: Brooding Hills

desc:
These hills, lower than the rest of the surrounding ones, force you to view only
the greyish grass and bushes that grow here. All of your instincts tell you that
something is very wrong here, and to leave as soon as possible.
name: Brooding Hills

desc:
A wide, deep, green-covered valley descends southeastwards, to the encounter of
the snaking waters of the Bruinen. A range of overlapping, small shoulders form
this majestic, northern edge. Their worn peaks look like half-ruined battlements
in a gigantic wall that cuts the icy, northerly wind off from the fertile valley
floor. Further south, the other wall rises timidly above the plains that mark
the landscape in the distance.
name: Northern Wall of a Green Valley

desc:
Sheltered from the frozen, devastating wind from the mountains, the bottom of
this deep vale looks in all its splendour. Here and there, small copses of leafy
trees stand, surrounded by short, thick, wet grass growing all about. The murmur
of running water mingles with the soft whisper of the fluttering, dark-green
leaves, like an endless melody that repeats once and again. The dale opens as it
draws closer to the riverbed, as if trying to embrace it.
name: A Secluded, Green Valley

desc:
The course of the river leads mostly straight here, and the flow hastens. On the
eastern bank, a sheer cliff of bare rock raises above the waters, not that high
yet vertically. And it seems to flank the stream, like a motionless bodyguard of
cold stone. The waters pass by gently, almost noiselessly, along the grey wall,
as if softly caressing it.
name: Near the Meeting of the Branches

desc:
Looking through the limpid, crystalline water, one can clearly glimpse the rocky
bottom of the riverbed, littered in boulders of all sizes. At dusk, when the
westering sunlight falls aslant over the river, the mirror-like surface shines
in bright orange shades. But when the nights are clear, the silvery gleam of the
moon and the stars shimmers in the apparently asleep, whispering waters.
name: Bend in the North Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
Cold and quiet run the waters of the Bruinen, winding along the foothills of the
Misty Mountains, and opening their way through the abrupt valleys and the stony
crags. The elven people call this river Bruinen, but it was best known as
Loudwater among the men that inhabited these lands long ago. And so it is still
called by their descendants, although none of them dwell in this rough region
any longer.
name: The Twisting Bruinen

desc:
Even in the sunniest summer days, the waters always remain icy to the touch.
Upstream in the Misty Mountains, among recondite canyons, are the riverbasins of
the Bruinen. And their waters come from the thawing snow that crowns the
everlasting white peaks, that reach out towards the sky, like white fingers
trying to touch it.
name: A Bend in the North Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The terrain undulates in this area, with large outcrops of rock that appear to
spring forth out of the very ground, as if expelled by some unknown power. These
stony protuberances are more numerous the closer to the river, like a levee
built by the capricious forces of nature. In all other directions, the ridges
give way to small hillocks, and those to old, worn hills, their smooth
silhouettes outlined against the horizon.
name: Cragged River Bank

desc:
As the waters come down from the mountains, the flow becomes slower, although
mightier too. The frequent waterfalls and rapids have been left behind, as well
as the narrow gorges with upright walls of living rock. And so have the white
waters embroidered with spray, rushing through the sharp-edged stones of
capricious shapes. Here, among the foothills, the stream runs lazily and
quietly, as if taking a break after the thrilling descent.
name: North Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
In this section, the high cliffs of stone draw back on the eastern bank, as they
give way to a beach dotted with pebbles. Nevertheless, the tall stone walls do
not disappear, but they stand equally sheer a bit further inland, maybe hinting
a different course of the river in the past. With every gust of the wind, little
waves go and fade at the gravelly beach with a soft rubbing sound.
name: North Branch by a Gravelly Riverbank

desc:
This lonely hill rises sharply into the sky, giving you a decent view of the
rather uninspiring land. A huge forest attracts your attention to the north
east, giving you an eventual path to go and explore. From here, the only way to
go is back to the north, and follow the hills downwards to the lower area or
south into a green valley.
name: Brooding Hills

desc:
Though the hills all around are covered by tall grass and brush, the plants are
all of an unhealthy grey hue. Northward, the hills diminish, worn by the endless
passage of time. To the east, the hills rise and fall, like a sea of grey-green
plants, all rolling in the same tide. Southwest of here, a green valley seems to
beckon, offering perhaps a respite from these bleak hills, and to the west and
northwest, a massive forest dominates the land.
name: Brooding Hills

desc:
At this point, the hills are worn and aged. The same grey grass that covers the
rugged and brooding hills to the south is here, but not as thick. The huge
forest right to the west seems to wear down these hills, taking over the
immediate land. The hills are even more worn to the north, but they seem safer
than the overpowering forest.
name: Worn Hills

desc:
These hills are quite low, almost non-existent. The most prominent feature of
them being a part of the larger ones to the south is the bleached grass that
covers the ground here. The will of these hills is gone, destroyed by time and
the more recent pressures of the vast and incredible forest to the west.
name: Worn Hills

desc:
A trail picks its way carefully through the sparse vegetation growing stubbornly
from the rocky ground. The air is still and heavy, effectively sucking the life
out of the already dreary landscape. In the distance to the west, a large forest
lines the horizon. The trail effectively disappears to the east, leaving you to
find your own path to walk on.
name: Trail through the Moor

desc:
Forming a large rim, the bowl of rock here prevents you from looking out over
the canyon basin that is to the west. A path can be picked out that way, so with
a little effort, you can make your way there carefully. The centre of the bowl
is damp from the frequent drainage from the large rain trail to the south, but
the porous rock prevents you from taking a drink there.
name: Rocky Crater

desc:
This trail plunges steeply downwards into the rocky valley below. The only
reason you can travel here at all is because of the fact that the rains over the
years have carved a workable path through the stone itself, as it drains from
the fertile land to the south.
name: Rain Trail

desc:
On the northern edge of the forest, you notice the healthy if scattered trees
around here. This is where the forest ends and the rocky canyon region begins to
take over. Looking carefully, perhaps a trail exists to the north which is safe
enough to travel down, if one wishes to explore to the north.
name: A Sparse Forest

desc:
This northern edge of the forest is comprised of tall and healthy spruce, oak,
and pine trees. Though the trees themselves are loosely growing here, the fallen
leaves are sufficient to provide a blanket that covers the ground and softens
your footsteps.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. There are no signs of wildlife. The air is still and sombre.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
On the western edge of the moor, you see the beginnings of a large canyon to the
west. Everywhere else is just more of the same morose land. The air, calm and
dry, hangs heavily over this uninspiring land.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Exploring along the western edge of the moor, you walk along dry and infertile
land. All that grows here is weeds and other rank plant life. The air, despite
the presence of the Bruinen a bit to the east, is dry and calm, leaving a sense
of despair in the land.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Spreading out from the western side of the Bruinen, this western edge of the
moor presents a depressing view. Occasional vegetation juts up from the rocky
terrain, despite the size of the nearby river. To the east, you see more of the
same land and vegetation. A large canyon lies far to the west.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
On and on, the plains lead northwards. Much further are the infamous Ettenmoors
whose name has been known to fill many stout hearts with fear. No sounds of
wildlife can be heard here, with not even a breeze to move the tall, dry grass
that grows all around you.
name: Silent Plains

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation in
earthen colours. The travelling is slow with no paths to follow, bounded by some
impassable shrub to the north. You hear the sound of flowing water coming from
the east.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough,
covered with wildly sprawling vegetation that makes the going difficult. Here
the moors slope down to meet the river Bruinen to the east, making a passable
ford to the fertile plains on the other side.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough as
it gradually slopes down from its heights to the level of the fertile plain
across the river to the east. The travelling is difficult due to the sprawling
brush and undergrowth.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation in
earthen colours. The moors stretch northwards and eastwards to a spur of the
river Bruinen.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions but west, though the wildly sprawling vegetation
makes travelling a bit difficult. The air is still and sombre except for a faint
sound of rushing water from the east. To the west a sparse forest starts up,
populated thinly by healthy spruce oak and pine trees.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat, towering over the river which bends its way north at the eastern
edge of the moor. Across the river hills rise in response, but flatten out
further to the north into a fertile plain.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions, overgrown with wildly sprawling vegetation in
earthen colours. The moors stretch northward bounded only by the gurgling
Bruinen to the east.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions, covered with wildly sprawling earthen vegetation.
The going is hard and the only sounds are that of rushing water far to the east.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are at the southern edge of the Bruinen moor which stretches northwards as
far as you can see. To the west and south runs a valley whose floor is several
tree-heights below you. To the east you can hear the river running and you can
see the majestically soaring natural arch that spans it, yearning to reach the
sky.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat in all directions, hard to travel through due to the wild
overgrowth. To the east the rushing of the river can be heard, the only sound in
the sombre air.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are at the southern edge of the high Bruinen moor which stretches northwards
as far as you can see. The moor towers over the grassy valley below, higher even
then the forest to the west. Eastwards, over the river, a majestically soaring
natural rock arch spans across to the moors on the other side.
name: Edge of the Moor

desc:
This lonely hill, covered with a small wood, stands between the dark forest and
the river. Here stands a copse, comprised of obviously ancient oak trees, whose
gnarled roots stand proud of the ground in places. Four miles east of here an
archway can be seen soaring into the sky. It joins the moors on either side of
the river. Far to the east, the towering Misty Mountains can be seen, with their
many snow-covered peaks.
name: Hill in the Moor

desc:
This stretch of high moors is bounded to the east by the river Bruinen, which
flows along a gorge several feet below. Though possible, it would be dangerous
to leap into the rushing waters, and the current appears strong enough to sweep
you several miles downstream. Northwards a small bluff overlooks the river,
where a majestic rock arch soars high into the sky, bridging the moorland to
either side of the watercourse.
name: Edge of the Bruinen Moors

desc:
After walking past the archway through a rather flat area, you find yourself on
the top of a small cliff. Looking down from the top there is the riverbed of the
Bruinen. The impetuous current of the river has dug a cliff in the soft ground,
not more than fifteen feet high. A sheer cliff rises east and west from the
riverbed, but is smaller to the south.
name: Cliff over the Bruinen

desc:
A low ridge rises by the stream, overlooking its course for many miles. Its top
looks mostly level, as if flattened by the abundant rains in the region
throughout countless years. However, there are small hollows and holes in the
rock, where dark water gathers in small ponds. Nevertheless, the crag bends over
the waters to the northwest, like a half-built bridge, or maybe merely like a
thick pillar of stone whose base has been thrashed by the troubled waters of the
river.
name: An Arching Outcrop of Rock

desc:
The terrain continues to ascend, from the valley floor through which the river
flows up to some escarp bluffs towering in the distant east. The ground is
mostly covered in patches of thick, tussocky grass. There are small copses of
high firs as well, their pointy tops climbing up to the sky. Their dark-green
leaves shake in the wind, filling the dale with a deep whisper. A faint scent
emanates from those leaves, impregnating the air.
name: Lightly-forested Incline

desc:
Here too, the crystalline current of the Bruinen runs unapproachable along a
deep cutting several feet below. The ground drops steeply in an uneven wall of
rock dampened by the mists that hang over the water. Brambles and briars fill
any tiny gap among the stones covered in thick, green moss. Below, the waters
flow swiftly, singing loudly as they pass by the rock faces.
name: Rocky Riverbank

desc:
Always snaking among the briars and thorns, the track goes on imperturbably
along the slope. At some spots it is no more than a half-faded trail, hardly
distinguishable among the weeds. But in other places it looks rather better
marked, with small white stones lying on both waysides. The countless turns and
the thick brushwood make it hard to figure the course, but here it seems to bend
sharply from south to east.
name: Bend in the Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
This section of the trail skirts around a wide depression to the south, where
the ground becomes steep and slippery. Eastwards the terrain, the colour of
heather and crumbling rock, rises slowly toward the feet of the mountains. The
long line of the Bruinen can be seen glittering among banks of grey stone away
to the north.
name: Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
The track meanders lazily amongst ivy-clad hillocks, following an east-west
route. A yawning fissure opens to the south, dark and deep, so that one could
neither jump over nor climb down into it. Past the fissures a ridge of hills
rise, its heights broken into many bare points almost like the teeth of a
blunted saw.
name: Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
In all these moors there is no sign of any dwelling: wild and tangled vegetation
is all that covers the slightly undulating ground which stretches unchanged for
miles in all directions. Half covered with moss or heather, small white stones
show now and then where little footpaths depart from the main track. Most of
them lead only to a dead-end among the hillocks though.
name: Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are now in the southern side of a wide vale. At times, thick mists like
clouds of greyish vapour spread all over from the river at the bottom. But when
the days are clear, one can easily see the ground ascending steadily eastwards
for several miles, climbing up to some high, imposing moors. And there, among
the towering bluffs, the dale narrows slowly into a gorge through which the
Bruinen river flows like a trickle of silvery water.
name: In an Open Valley

desc:
As almost all along the southern fork of the Bruinen, the river flows between
cliff-like walls of rock, as if confined within a moat many feet below. Here the
bank appears to be only slightly less steep. However, it may be possible to
attempt a risky descent among the dangerously slippery rocks that spring from
the very waters, like bulky sentinels of stone flanking the stream.
name: Abrupt Riverside

desc:
A flat shelf paved with bare stone slopes very steeply into the Bruinen here,
bearing the marks of aeons of erosion. A cliff borders the river not far to the
east, although further to the south it gradually lowers. Thick brush can be seen
peeking over the clifftop, and creepers cling to the wall in cracks and holes.
name: Rocky River Bank

desc:
The loud rippling of the water opening its way through the rocks, and the
numerous, small falls command one's attention in this mostly straight section of
the river. Both banks look extraordinarily steep, like a little gorge with walls
of damp, living rock. The current is fast and foamy, but one can easily make out
the rock-sprinkled streambed. Lots of leaves, sticks, and even small stones
float by, carried away with the waters.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The river runs westward here, clear and chilly between steep banks of grey
stone. A few miles to the east a cliff rises up to flank the water, and further
upstream the river disappears, swallowed in a vast rift among the hills. A green
meadow spreads northward, until the foot of a ridge of rocky, bare hills that
loom far in the distance. Downstream the river is joined by the other fork of
the Bruinen, which flows in from the north.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
A sheer cliff to the north flanks the south fork of the Bruinen, which flows
steadily past a few dozen yards below. Crossing the area is difficult as matted
undergrowth covers the ground - brambles and creepers growing to the height of a
man's waist - and not a single footpath can be found in this corner of the
moors.
name: High Moors

desc:
A few barely noticeable tracks tread the moors, twisting through the thick
vegetation. Most of them lead only to a dead end though, or to the cliff edge
which overhangs a bend of the Bruinen. The river comes down from out of a rocky
gorge to the north, foaming and churning, then slows and turns westward.
name: High Moors

desc:
To the north grass and weeds grow on a muddy bank, while on the other side the
water is bordered by a tall cliff, which gradually lowers to the west. It was
carved by the river over thousands of years, and still the process is going on:
deep hollows form at its base and roots hang at its top, being exposed by the
crumbling terrain. A few miles east the river seems to spew from a gorge in the
moors.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The water flows at speed through the gorge, eddying around a few large boulders
that lie on its bed. It comes down with some speed from the north, and turns
west under the shadow of the high cliffs, losing speed as the river widens. A
few pine trees cling to the gorge walls on unreachable shelves halfway up, their
roots hanging out from the rock.
name: River Bruinen

desc:
This is the western edge of the moors, surrounded by the river to the north,
west, and south, like a last outpost of the elevated lands. In contrast, the
bluffs extend eastwards in a long procession as far as the eye can reach. An
abrupt cliff to the north and west frowns over the upper branch of the Bruinen,
while the lower fork glitters below, directly to the south. The cliffs look
crumbling and broken, towering upright above the unreachable waters.
name: Edge of the Moors

desc:
Although the moor isn't as high here as a bit further eastwards, there is still
quite a drop down to the troubled waters of the Bruinen, to the south. Away over
the river, a steep bank gives way to a markedly ascending ground. The raised
flatlands stretch away in all other directions, but on the north and mainly the
west they do so only for a couple miles, until they meet the bordering course of
the river.
name: High above the River

desc:
A green meadow lies between the forks of the Bruinen to the south and west.
Among the grass and flowers, a few tall fir trees rise near the stony banks,
nourished by the river waters. The terrain becomes poor and dry at the top of
the high moors that rise northward, and only a few heather plants manage to grow
there.
name: Grassy River Bank

desc:
The terrain slopes gently to the south, down to the river where the tracks of
small animals can be seen among the grass and weeds that grow in the soft mud.
Eastwards the bank rises higher and the impetuous current foams in the rocky
bed, pressed on by towering walls of stone. The river leaves your sight further
up the valley, where it comes roaring in from the north.
name: River Bank

desc:
As the valley narrows again the river churns and hurdles over rocks, and you are
flung and battered in almost every direction. Water is everywhere, and the force
of it is irresistible. You are hurtled downstream at increasing speed. At one of
the few points you are above water you catch a brief glimpse of the steep walls
of the gorge, and realise that the only way out is downstream.
name: Rapids in the River Bruinen

desc:
The high moor comes to a sudden end in an escarp cliff above the river, as
though the very water had cut it like a knife with a frozen, shifting blade.
Here the ground shows an ashen grey colour, and pebbles abound. But hardly
anything manages to grow here, except the occasional brownish heather. A bit off
south, the elevated bluff extends westwards, like a long finger of cold, barren
stone.
name: Atop a Cliff in the Moors

desc:
This is the western edge of a ridge of rocky hills that extends eastward in a
long procession as far as your sight can reach. An abrupt cliff to the west
frowns over the upper branch of the Bruinen, while the lower fork glitters far
away to the south. The cliff is crumbling and broken to the north, falling
towards a pebbled beach a many dozen yards below.
name: Edge of the Moors

desc:
All around here lies a rough and folded land; thick vegetation sprawls wildly
and not a copse or a solitary tree rises to break the undulating line of the
horizon. Southwards the Bruinen cleaves the country in a wide gorge, and near
its banks, the land becomes less harsh - grass, weeds, and green bushes grow
there on the sloping ground.
name: High Moors

desc:
A wide stretch of terrain lies between the river and the cliff that marks the
western edge of the high moors. In ages past it was the bed of the river, when
the rock was carved smooth and sheer by the water. Southwards the cliff is
broken and crumbling, yet it seems not possible to find a way up.
name: Gravelly River Bank

desc:
This is the bank of the Bruinen, which runs just to the west of here. The river
is wide and deep, making it a formidable barrier. No plant life grows on the
pebbled ground that is the river bank, but a few weeds can be seen at the base
of a cliff that slopes very steeply up to the east. The bank proceed further
southward.
name: Gravelly River Bank

desc:
This elevated place provides a good view over the course of the river: it flows
many hundred feet below between pebbled beaches, forming a wide arc from the
north to the west. A huge fissure opens eastward in the rocky and barren soil,
its flanks made of dark crumbling rock.
name: Cliff over the Bruinen

desc:
The north fork of the Bruinen cleaves its way through the moors west of here,
with high cliffs flanking its course northward for many miles. A few withered
plants grow in this windswept place, while eastward, where the ground dips in a
shallow dell, bushes and high grass cover the terrain.
name: Cliff over the Bruinen

desc:
Small footpaths tread over the brush-covered hills that spread in almost every
direction from here. Their journey is erratic and studded with forks though, and
most of them lead only to a sheer cliff to the south. At the bottom of the gorge
the Bruinen hurries along, sprinkling and foaming around the stones, and the
rushing of water echoes up from the valley. Imposing spurs of rock thrust
southwards from the cliff, screening the valley further upstream from your view.
name: High Moors

desc:
The ground dips to form a shallow depression, filled with roots, bushes, and
rocks hidden among the high grass. Here and there some huge boulder sticks out
of the vegetation, probably fallen from the crumbling flanks of a large cleft to
the north. The rising ground blocks your sight in every direction; maybe there
is a better view at the top of a hillock that rises east of here.
name: Dip in the Moors

desc:
You are in a huge cleft in the hills, well below the level of the surrounding
land. To the east a scree climbs up to the moors, looking like a river of white
stones. The bottom of the rift here is studded with broken rocks, fallen from
the scree or the cleft walls. Still some vegetation, mainly whin and brambles,
manages to grow in the dry soil.
name: Crumbling Rift

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. There are no signs of wildlife. The area here dips below the level of the
hills and you cannot make out much of your surrounding area, although to the
south you can see a crumbled rift in the moors.
name: Bruinen Moor Hollow

desc:
The river Bruinen runs northeasterly-southwesterly through the moors which rise
up on the east side of the bank. To the west a sudden copse of trees appears.
The air is still and quiet, punctuated only by an occasional low howl from the
moors.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river Bruinen runs northeasterly-southwesterly through the moors which rise
up to great heights on either side of the crystal waters. The air is still and
quiet, punctuated only by an occasional low howl from the moors.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river Bruinen runs from the northeast to the southwest through the moors.
The smooth, crystal water flows silently, the only sound being an occasional
howl from beyond the shores of the moors.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
A small grove of trees has sprung up here fighting with the moors to the north
and the river to the east for survival. The trees are densely packed and provide
excellent protection from the elements. Huge, gnarled roots extend down and into
the river to the east.
name: A Copse

desc:
The river Bruinen runs northeasterly-southwesterly through the moors, rising up
on either side of the crystal waters. The air is still and quiet, punctuated
only by an occasional low howl from the moors.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
You have reached a bluff overlooking the river Bruinen, at the foot of a
majestic rock arch reaching skyward and spanning the running waters. The arch
seems an easily crossable bridge; caution must be taken while crossing, however,
since it is quite a height to the flowing river below... The river flows
north-south, splitting into two branches north of here. On this side of the
river sprawls more of the Bruinen moors.
name: Bruinen River Bluff

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough but
mostly flat, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation. The river lies to the
east, many tree-heights below. Southwards a small bluff overlooks the river,
where a majestic rock arch soars into the sky joining the moors on either side
of the river.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the high moors overlooking the Bruinen which flows
southward many tree-heights below. The land is rough but mostly flat, covered
with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover. Across the river
matching cliffs rise out of the fertile plains.
name: Flat Bruinen Moor

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen enters a turn here around some hills, flowing from the
north to the east. The waters churn over the rocky streambed, carving their way
through the limestone that form the walls of the moor and cliffs to either side
of the river.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen flows gently from the north, past some hills to the
east and the high moors to the west. The only sound is the gurgling of the river
further to the south.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen flows gently from the north, bordering a fertile plain
to the east and the moors to the west. The moors slope down to the water's edge
and the river is fordable here as it flows over a gravel bed, thus making travel
from the plain to the moors possible.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen flows gently from the north, bordering a fertile plain
to the east and the high moors to the west. The air is muted and all around is
silent, befitting the stark landscape around you.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
The fertile plain beneath your feet runs wildly into the distance, bounded only
by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the southwest and southeast. Grass joyfully
grows tall here, nourished by the waters of the river. Something seems to rise
out of the flat earth to the east.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The plain rises slightly here, forming some low hills terminating in a bluff
overlooking the river south of here. From this height, you can see the plain
stretching northwards to where the west spur of the North Bruinen curves back
eastward.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The plain rises slightly here, forming some low hills terminating in a bluff
overlooking the river south of here. From this height, you can see the plain
stretching endlessly northwards.
name: Low Hills

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen turns around some northern hills, flowing from the west
to the south. The waters churn over the rocky streambed, carving their way
through the limestone that form the substrata of most of this region.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
Unexpectedly a mound rises here out of the earth. Covered with grass, you can
nevertheless make out the unmistakable rusted relics of a hard-fought battle of
a bygone era scattered here and there. You realise with a start that this must
be the burial mound of the slain and shudder when you think that this plain is
not only fertile by the waters of the Bruinen but also by these dead.
name: Burial Mound

desc:
The fertile plain sprouts joyfully growing grass beneath your feet, racing
wildly into the distance, bounded only by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the
southwest and southeast. To the west the landscape burps, for a small earthen
mound rises out of the surrounding flatness.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The fertile plain beneath your feet runs wildly into the distance, bounded only
by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the southwest and southeast. Grass joyfully
grows tall here, nourished by the waters of the river. It is a wonder that this
land has not been cultivated as you savor the richness of life and air.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The fertile plain beneath your feet runs wildly into the distance, bounded only
by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the southwest and southeast. Grass joyfully
grows tall here, nourished by the waters of the river. This seems like a good
place to have a picnic with the river gently flowing by.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs
southeasterly toward the vale of Imladris. A fertile plain bounds the river to
the north and a rolling heath lies to the south from which an occasional
rustling and cluck startle the air.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs
southeasterly toward the vale of Imladris. A fertile plain bounds the river to
the north and a rolling heath lies to the south.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs
southeasterly toward the vale of Imladris. A fertile plain bounds the river to
the north and a rolling heath lies to the south, from which cluck-clucking may
be heard.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs
southeasterly toward the vale of Imladris. A fertile plain bounds the river to
the north and a rolling heath lies to the south.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river skirts some cliffs to the west as it continues to meander southwest,
passing the bleak Bruinen heath to the east. The air is still and quiet, though
occasionally a cluck-cluck can be heard above the trickling waters.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river skirts some cliffs to the west as it continues to meander southwest,
passing the bleak Bruinen heath to the east. The air is still and quiet, though
occasionally a cluck-cluck can be heard above the trickling waters.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river skirts some cliffs to the west as it continues to meander southwest,
passing the bleak Bruinen heath to the east. The air is still and quiet, though
occasionally a cluck-cluck can be heard above the trickling waters.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
This spur of the Bruinen flows from the north toward a fork in the river. Cliffs
overshadow the river to the east and you can make out the faint outline of what
seems to be a bridge of some sort arching over the river further to the south.
name: West Spur of North Bruinen

desc:
The river skirts some cliffs to the north as it continues to meander southwest,
passing the bleak Bruinen heath to the east. The air is still and quiet, though
occasionally a cluck-cluck can be heard above the trickling waters.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
Here two branches of the North Bruinen come together at the foot of some tall
cliffs. One spur heads straight towards the north, while the other flows towards
the northeast and the peaks of the Misty Mountains. Both join into a larger
river as it flows south under a huge natural stone arch.
name: Fork in North Bruinen

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs to join a
western spur to the south, where can also be seen the outline of a great rock
arch or bridge spanning the moors on either side of the river.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
A majestic natural rock span arches overhead this stretch of the Bruinen,
casting a pale shadow upon the rippling crystal waters. The span joins the
eastern and western moors, both of which rise up on either side of the river.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river Bruinen takes a slow southwesterly course, from under an arching rock
formation to the north. The waters cut through the moors here, etching and
delving through the limestone.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
The river has carved a great stone arch. It looks quite sturdy and wide enough
for safe travel. The arch itself seems to be one huge piece of rock carved by
the relentless rushing of the Bruinen. Perhaps in the past some disturbance
raised this temporary block to the river, which was soon remedied. Now it serves
as an excellent path over the river.
name: A Natural Bridge

desc:
You have reached a northerly bluff overlooking the fork of the Bruinen where two
spurs join into one and flow under a soaring natural rock arch to the south
which spans the moors on either side of the river. To the north, the fertile
plain stretches indefinitely, nourished by the flowing waters. To the east, you
can enter the strong current of the Bruinen.
name: Bruinen River Bluff

desc:
The plain rises slightly here, forming some low hills terminating in a bluff
overlooking the river south of here. From this height, you can see the plain
stretching endlessly northwards.
name: Low Hills

desc:
The fertile plain beneath your feet runs wildly into the distance, bounded only
by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the southwest and southeast. Grass joyfully
grows tall here, nourished by the waters of the river. A small mound of earth
seems to rise a little further north but nothing compared to the low hills of
the south.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The fertile plain beneath your feet runs wildly into the distance, bounded only
by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the southwest and southeast. Grass joyfully
grows tall here, nourished by the waters of the river. Only some small hills to
the northwest and southwest disturb the horizon.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
The fertile plain sprouts joyfully growing grass beneath your feet, racing
wildly into the distance, bounded only by the two spurs of the Bruinen to the
southwest and southeast. Unfortunately, the river bank is also full of grass,
preventing fording the river to the brown heath on the other side.
name: Fertile Plain

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The silence is broken only by an occasional cluck from
somewhere in the dry ground cover. The heath stretches in all directions, shaped
like an oval, bounded by what sounds like the river Bruinen in the west.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The silence is broken only by an occasional cluck or rustle
from somewhere in the dry ground cover. The heath stretches in all directions,
ovally shaped, bounded by what sounds like the river Bruinen in the west.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The silence is broken only by an occasional cluck from
somewhere in the dry ground cover. The heath stretches in all directions,
seemingly blown by the west wind and moulded by the river Bruinen.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The silence is broken only by an occasional cluck from
somewhere in the dry ground cover. The heath stretches from the branch of the
Bruinen to the north, rippling land onward to the brooding moors which rise up
against the horizon further south.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. You feel unbounded by the constraints of space as you spread
your arms to encompass the golden heath, and your mind roams freely over the
gently rolling lay of the land.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. Only occasional clucking disturb the brooding atmosphere. The
heath stretches in all directions, bounded by what sounds like the river Bruinen
in the west and what seems to be even hillier moors to the south.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
This bluff overlooks the fork of the Bruinen where two tributaries merge into
one. South of the fork a majestic rock formation arches into the sky, spanning
the river. To the northeast you see the rolling lands of the heath and to the
south sprawl the hilly moors, covered with scrub and pockmarked with dips and
hollows.
name: Bruinen River Bluff

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The only visible landmark is the natural arch over the Bruinen to the
northwest for most of moor is nondescript, pockmarked with little hollows.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover which
makes passage difficult. The area here dips below the level of the hills around
you and you cannot make out much of your surrounding area. The hill to your
northwest seems to be taller than the rest, though.
name: Bruinen Moor Hollow

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, even
tougher than the brush of the heath. The air is clear and you can see the land
dipping and rising around you, even northwards to that great span of rock that
arches over the Bruinen.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
A narrow path leads you through the high, broken moors between the two branches
of the Bruinen. It winds among the hills and descends slowly following a south-
east course. A few patches of grass and small twisted bushes grow in the nearly
barren ground.
name: A Rough Trail

desc:
This is the edge of a long ridge of hills that extends westward rising and
falling until the horizon. Southwards a sheer fall of many hundreds of feet
frowns over a dark and wide gorge. On the other side of the ravine a ridge
crowned with windswept firs rises against the sky. Eastward the land is broken -
cliffs, crevices, and ravines block your way and your view.
name: Edge of the Moors

desc:
A footpath runs over a level strip of terrain, under the cover of the sheer
cliff of dark rock which rises several hundred feet high to the west. The ground
is encumbered with many boulders fallen from the cliff walls, dotting the green
of the grass. The land ascends gently eastward, away from the cliff, and the
meadow gradually gives ground to a forest of pine-trees.
name: A Rough Trail

desc:
Coming from the elevated terrain in the west, the trail bends southward. It
borders on the outskirts of a pine forest that climbs a slope to the east, where
it grows thicker and darker. The vegetation is thick enough to partly hide the
track; here and there roots hinder your walk.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation. You are on a stretch of higher
land, with dips and hollows here and there off to the side. The going is tough
because of the undergrowth and perhaps that's why you see no signs of wildlife.
The air here is still and sombre.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover that
makes travelling difficult. There are little signs of wildlife. The visible
landmark is the natural arch over the Bruinen to the northwest, though from here
you can barely make it out, slender as it is.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
The small footpath you have been trying to follow disappears into a tangle of
impenetrable brush here on the side of the hills. The land rises up sharply
around you and there seems no way of climbing the steep sides to get a better
view of the lay of the land.
name: Small Footpath

desc:
You are trying to follow a small footpath into the brooding moors. The path
winds through the golden heath around and amongst the rolling hills. It seems
but slightly used as none of the surrounding vegetation seems to be disturbed.
name: Small Footpath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. To the east there is a small rise and beyond that a small
stretch of trail that winds upwards into the Misty Mountains. The heath,
however, rolls unbounded northwards, as if the land were moved and rippled by
the blowing wind.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
Standing on this rise you can see the golden heath stretching out before you to
the north and to the west, with the Bruinen river at its furthest end. South
sprawls the moors of the Bruinen, rough and hilly. A small footpath seems to
lead downward and southwestward into its depths.
name: Small Rise

desc:
You are trying to follow a small footpath into the moors. The path winds and
snakes its way through the heath around and amongst the rolling hills. It seems
but slightly used as little of the surrounding vegetation seems to be disturbed
though you spot some telltale traces here and there of some woods- crafty
wanderer.
name: Small Footpath

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover which
makes the going rough. Somewhere to the south, you know, lies the vale of
Imladris, or as men call it, Rivendell.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Some sparse young pine trees rise at the foot of a grassy slope, before a huge
forest spreading eastward behind them as far as your sight can reach. Various
bushes grow in hollows and among the few rocks that stick out of the grass. To
the west a ridge of bleak hills looms in the distance.
name: Outskirts of a Pine Forest

desc:
As the terrain slowly ascends eastward, the pines become thicker and thicker.
Grass covers the terrain among the trees, and huge erratic stone blocks lie
broken and half buried. To the west a small path runs at the foot of a sheer
cliff wall before crossing the south fork of the Bruinen, where the stream
spreads in stony shoals.
name: Outskirts of a Pine Forest

desc:
For thousands of years the water carved the smooth walls that enclose and
shelter this great bay. Now however, the ground is completely covered by pebbles
and rocks, as the river takes a different route to the south. A few trees and
some matted undergrowth manage to grow under the shadow of the east side, where
some loam has fallen from the cliff. To the north the cliff has better endured
the challenge of time, and is still smooth and impassable.
name: Dried-up Bend of the Bruinen

desc:
Sparse patches of grass and heather plants strive for life in these bleak and
broken hills; against the odds it would appear. A great bay enclosed by steep
cliffs opens suddenly to the south, many hundreds of feet below. Carved long ago
by the water which has long since departed for another route, these days only a
stream of pebbles paves its bed. The cliff here falls smooth and sheer, allowing
no descent, but elsewhere it is cracked and crumbled.
name: High Moors

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. You are in a small dell, surrounded by higher moorland on all sides.
Here, not a sound stirs the air... you feel you are almost a part of the silent,
brooding moor.
name: Bruinen Moor Hollow

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The only visible landmark is the natural arch over the Bruinen to the
northwest for most of moor is nondescript, pockmarked with little hollows.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover that make
travelling difficult. The land dips down sharply to the west and south though
you can try to keep on the high land. You can espy the top of the natural arch
over the Bruinen to the northwest from here, soaring even higher than you stand
now.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry and
impassable as it is. The only visible landmark is the natural arch over the
Bruinen to the west, though from here you can't really discern the sounds of the
river. Northwards roll the gentler heath.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Here the rolling heath from the north meet the more hilly moors to the south.
There is no marked change in vegetation, the heath turning into more dense and
more brownish brush. The land dips and rises more sharply in the moors though,
and you are careful watching your step. The only sound in the air is the
gurgling of running water close by.
name: Edge of the Moors

desc:
You have reached a bluff overlooking the river Bruinen, at the foot of a
majestic rock arch reaching skyward and spanning the running waters. It seems an
easily passable bridge. It is quite a height to the flowing river below... To
the northeast sprawls the heath and to the south lie the moors from which you
came.
name: Bruinen River Bluff

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The area here dips below the level of the hills and you cannot make out
much of the lay of the land, but you think you can make out that great natural
arch just northwest of here.
name: Bruinen Moor Hollow

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The only visible landmark is the natural arch over the Bruinen to the
northwest for most of moor is nondescript, pockmarked with little hollows.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The only visible landmark is the natural arch over the Bruinen to the
northwest for most of moor is nondescript, pockmarked with little hollows.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Thick heather covers the low, rocky hills that spread for many miles to the
south and east. Westwards a small scree descends steeply into a deep rift,
enclosed between crumbling cliff walls. No visible path crosses this country,
and in all the silent moors there is no sign of any dwelling.
name: High Moors

desc:
The ridge of rocky heather covered hills that extends eastward and northward for
miles comes to a sudden end: a great bay, enclosed by steep cliffs and paved
with round pebbles, lies to the east many yards below. It was the ancient bed of
the river, which later chose a straighter course. A huge rocky spur thrusts
southward, wrapped in thin mist, and from that direction the faint rumble of a
waterfall can be heard.
name: High Moors

desc:
A great heel of rock thrusts outward from the cliffs, overhanging a lofty
waterfall which rumbles in the deep gorge far below. Thin mist from the falls
surrounds the place and veils the landscape all around you. Still, a wide
bowl-shaped valley, as opposed to the gorge here, can be guessed westward. On
the other side of the fall a cliff rises again, and a dark-green line of trees
follows its edge.
name: Massive Spur above the Waterfall

desc:
The flat top of a high hillock here allows a good view of the surrounding land:
the undulating ground stretches unchanged for miles and miles, covered only
sparsely by heather and small withered bushes. A crumbling fall opens to the
north, and a stream of white stones can be seen down there, descending
westwards.
name: High Moors

desc:
At the bottom of a widening rift a white stream of rocks slopes steeply to the
west, flanked to the south by a crumbling cliff wall. It is quite unstable, and
even the fall of a small pebble could result in a landslide. It is very tiring
to walk on the scree in any direction, as the stones give way at every step.
name: A Small Scree

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The brambles of the brush are tough and hinder your passage. The only
visible landmarks are the natural arch over the Bruinen to the north, and the
scree of white stones to the south.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The area here dips below the level of the hills and you cannot make out
much of your surrounding area, save the sound of gurgling water somewhere close
by to the west.
name: Bruinen Moor Hollow

desc:
The great rocky sides of the ravine draw nearer, as the stream bends southward
and becomes faster and deeper. From there a faint but distant rumbling sound can
be heard. Over time the water eroded away the rock to the north, making a great
bay, now dried up, that opens in the sheer northern walls.
name: Bend in the Bruinen

desc:
The stream flows shallow and broad on a wide expanse of pebbles, between two
towering walls of rock knife-edged against the sky far above. Little light
reaches the bottom, even on sunny days, and dark moss covers the lowest part of
the walls. Midway to the top a few scrawny trees grow in crevices and fissures.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The river widens to many hundreds of feet here in stony shoals, trickling and
bubbling over a bed of shining pebbles. To the west it threads its way into a
deep, wide gorge where tall cliffs rise like mighty towers on either side. A
little trail climbs the grassy banks of the river, keeping close to the cliff.
name: Ford in the Bruinen

desc:
At the bottom of an open valley the river runs slowly, before gradually widening
westward and dividing into many small streamlets as the river passes through a
ford. To the north many sparse pines rise from the grass, and the forest becomes
denser the further up the slopes you look. A green meadow lies on the southern
bank - a small open area before further forest.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The stream flows under the shadow of a pine forest to the north, so that much of
the river is overhung by their branches. The shoreline rises and falls as
V-shaped gullies end by the river, their sides cut by the water. A gentle slope
climbs to the south, and grass and weeds grow on its muddy bank.
name: South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The ground slopes steeply southward, towards the south fork of the Bruinen which
flows under the shadow of the trees. The land is cut by V-shaped gullies,
created by the many small creeks that run down to the river. Some grey lichen
clings to the pines, but no plant life or undergrowth manages to grow under the
dark shadow of the trees.
name: Dense Pine Forest

desc:
The trees struggle silently for light in this thick forest, and the trunks rise
tall and slender. Their bark has a fierce red-brown colour and are completely
bare almost till the top, where they spread into a mushroom crown of twisted and
gnarled boughs. A thick and soft carpet of pine needles conceals the sloping
ground, and every now and then an uprooted old tree slowly rots on the ground.
name: Dense Pine Forest

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry and
deader than usual. Around you rise the higher lands of the moor, almost brooding
in their stillness and silence.
name: Bruinen Hollow

desc:
You are meandering through the moors of the Bruinen. The land is rough and
hilly, covered with wildly sprawling vegetation and thick ground cover, dry as
it is. The edge of the moor is just northwards where it encounters the heath.
From here, you can see a great forest dominating the skyline to the east,
however, it is unreachable from here as the roughness of the moorland makes
travelling difficult.
name: Bruinen Moor

desc:
Standing on this rise you can see the rolling heath stretching out before you to
the north and to the west, with the Bruinen river at its furthest end. South
sprawls the moors of the Bruinen, rough and hilly. A small footpath seems to
lead downward and another can be seen to the east.
name: Small Rise

desc:
The trail here bends south to east, clipping the golden Bruinen heath to the
northwest, where occasional cluck-cluckings permeate the stillness of the air. A
small footpath disappears westward into the hills.
name: Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The heath stretches west and south, bounded only by what
sounds like the river Bruinen to the north and rising land to the east upon
which a trail to the Misty Mountains climbs ever upward.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
You are wandering in the heath, a rolling expanse of country covered with earth
yellow underbrush. The silence is broken only by an occasional cluck from
somewhere in the dry ground cover. The heath stretches southward toward the
moors from the river flowing just north of here.
name: Bruinen Heath

desc:
Flowing from its source in the Misty Mountains, the river Bruinen runs
southeasterly toward the vale of Imladris. A fertile plain bounds the river to
the north and a rolling expanse of heath lies to the south.
name: North Bruinen

desc:
As you climb up the hills, a number of rocks fall free and roll down the hill.
It's hard to keep a steady footing, and no tracks of your passage are left
behind. You notice a small hole in the rocks to the south, but it is too small
for one of your size.
name: Hills

desc:
The path takes another turn here, turning from the east to the north. The hills
surrounding the path are relatively small compared to the mountains that can be
seen off in the distance. It seems hard to believe this path can take you all
the way to those mountains.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
As the path continues on, so do the deep ruts. The path here takes a curve
winding towards the east. To the north is a small patch of trees, but there is
no obvious entrances from this side.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The higher moor tapers away here, giving way to the gentler rolling heath
northwards. This area seems to be frequented with travellers as it is close to
the trail to the Misty Mountains and seems to be less wild than the rest of the
moors. The air, however, is silent.
name: Edge of the Moor

desc:
You are standing in a small grove of trees that provide comfortable shade for a
weary traveller. To the east is the main road, and you can hear the sound of a
river nearby.
name: Small Patch of Trees

desc:
As the Bruinen continues, you find yourself dozing off on your boat. The wide,
steady river flows onward in a roundabout fashion. To the south is a clump of
trees, and to the north are some plains. The river continues to the east and
west.
name: Bruinen

desc:
The path bends here, continuing upon its way to the west and to the north of
you. And as far as you can see in both directions, it bears deep ruts. To the
east of here the vast expanse of the Loudwater Plains opens up before you.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
The path continues onward, seemingly forever. It is easy to walk on the
hard-packed dirt, as long as you avoid the deep ruts that can occasionally be
found in the middle of the road. To the east are some hills, and the path
continues to the west and the south.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
The dirt path takes another curve here, going both north and west. The plains
are just opening up on the east, and you can spot many hills dotting them as
they spread out over the land.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
As the hard-packed path continues, another curve from the south towards the east
forms. The ruts here continue along the path, as if they follow it. To the west
is a small clump of trees, and to the north is a river.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The curve in the road turns again, going from the west to the north. To the east
and south are the plains, and farther on you can see more of the hills. Farther
north you can see the river again.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The road here is getting a bit softer, as it nears the river. The path takes a
turn from the south to the east, and to the north and west is the river. The
deep ruts continue along the road.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The road path here finally straightens out for a bit. The path continues west
and east, and the river continues along the path to the north. To the south are
the plains and the hills, and there is a wild patch of trees growing down there.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
As the path continues from the west to the east, so do the deep ruts. Some
evidence to what made these ruts are finally seen along the sides of the road as
there are a few wagon wheels stuck in the dirt, from when the river switched its
course.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
The tracks here seem more obvious, as the path gets slightly softer near the
river. This path seems to be a major trade highway. The path continues to the
west and east, with the river running alongside it to the north.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
The trade path starts winding again, as the river moves, and it follows the
river. To the north and west the path continues, and to the east and south are
some plains. Farther to the south you can see a wild forest.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
The path takes a bend here, heading both west and south. The path makes a slight
turn here to avoid some of the hills to the north. Along the sides of the roads
a few flowers grow by themselves.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
As you enter the hills, you find a small path that winds it's way through them,
making it easy to go through them. The main road runs around the hills, as the
bigger caravans cannot wind their way through these hills.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
You slowly trudge up this large hill. You look around and see that there are
many large rocks resting on the sides of the small trail you are on. The trail
leads from the west to the top of the hill to the north.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
The plains here become slightly hilly as they run into the hills to the north
and the south. There are no paths visible that would allow passage into the
hills. However, you see a break in the forest to the south, where the trees grow
close together and the undergrowth sprawls over the floor of the forest in
clumps and sheets of green vegetation.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
You are standing on the edge of some vast plains. There are a few trees growing
here and there, with a large concentration of trees towards the south. You don't
see any visible path into the forest to the south, as the trees are growing too
close together. To the northwest you see a small concentration of hills, and
there are also some hills to the northeast.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The trees are starting to get thicker here, as you enter the forest. The air is
filled with birds singing and the leaves of the oak trees rustling in the slight
breeze. In some places, the trees are so close that you cannot continue moving.
To the west the light grows brighter as you see the entrance of the forest, but
to the east the forest grows darker.
name: Outskirts of the Wild Forest

desc:
As you walk into the forest, the light grows dimmer, and the trees grow closer.
Untamed by man or elf, these trees have been growing wildly, as free as they
were designed to be. There seems to be quite a mixture of trees, including
mostly evergreens and a few oaks and maples scattered around. To the north are
the plains, and all around is more of the forest.
name: Entrance to the Wild Forest

desc:
The trees are starting to get thicker here, and are bunching together to block
movements in many directions. With this many trees, and in this state of
wildness, you have never felt closer to nature. To the east the light grows
stronger, and the only other way that movement is possible is to the south,
where the trees block out most of your vision.
name: Outskirts of the Wild Forest

desc:
The trail in the hills starts here, and leads up to the peak of the hill to the
west. The south is too littered with stones and rocks to be safe to travel into.
The vast Loudwater Plains can be seen to the north and east.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
You have reached the peak of this small peak. After a moments break, you stop to
take a look at the area surrounding you. To the east and south you can see a
large wild forest, where the trees grow so close that it looks like a landscape
made of green leaves. To the west you can see a small path that winds its way
far to the south and off into the distance to the north. For a while it runs
along the mighty river called the Bruinen.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
This small bit of grassland is isolated from most of the rest of the great
Loudwater Plains.Surrounded on the east and west by gently rolling hills, and
the north by the path, this bit of plains looks like a nice place to rest for a
while. There are a few old campfires seen here and there, long cold.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The grass here is thick, deep and luxuriant. You feel like you can fall down in
the grass and sleep for eternity. A large hill rises up to the south, but you
can see no safe path leading up it. The plains continue in all other directions,
including north to the river Bruinen, also known as Loudwater.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
As you make your way through the plains, you can see a small path leading up
into the hills to the south. This hill is the highest point around this area, so
you think that it might have a good view of the surrounding area. In all other
directions, the plains continue, as plains are often led to do.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The plains starting here are mostly flat, covered in a lush grass with a few
other grasses and weeds interwoven into the pattern. The great river Bruinen can
be seen a bit to the north, and the path that runs alongside it is to the north
and west. There are a few old campfires in this area, from caravans that stopped
here long ago. Now, weeds are growing amid the rocks and ashes.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The grassy plains continue on here, going in all directions but north. The grass
whispers against your legs as you make your way through it. To the north you can
see the path that runs along the Bruinen.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The plains here run into the road that runs with the river Bruinen. The grass
fans out in all the other directions, into a few hills that dot the landscape.
The grass is here is short, and looks like it has been grazed.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The plains take a break here as they run up the small hills to the north and
south. The bulk of the plains lie to the southwest, where a few small trees can
be seen growing here and there. To the north lies the small path.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
Here are a couple of small hills that rise up out of the plains. They are soft,
gentle hills with barely any height to them. The grass does not grow much up on
the hills, as they are covered in small rocks and pebbles. The hills continue on
to the south, and to the west are the Loudwater Plains.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
A couple of small hills stand here, giving you a better view of the surrounding
area. To the west you see a hill that stands higher than any of the others, and
you think the view would be better over there. The plains stretch out to the
west, towards that hill.
name: Rolling Hills

desc:
The dark forest starts to take turns now, as the trees grow in a seemingly
random pattern. The trees form barriers that are impossible to pass, so you must
travel in the few open spots. Travel is possible to the west and south.
name: Deep within the Wild Forest

desc:
As you come into this area of the forest, you can't help but seeing the
squirrels bouncing around from tree to tree playing some game that only they
understand. The forest here thins out a bit, and to the south you notice some
kind of clearing, where there are no trees at all.
name: Wild Forest

desc:
The trees here are becoming thicker as they merge into the large forest to the
south and east. You begin to hear the calls of some of the animals in the
forest, and you can hear the subtle rustling of movements deep within. There are
a few ranges of hills around you, to the west and also to the northeast.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
Here in the plains grow a few trees, randomly placed every now and then. The
trees grow much thicker to the south, as they grow into the wild forest that
lies before you. The ground here rises up on a slow slope to the east as it
leads up into the small hills.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
You here the gentle chirping of the small insects that inhabit this area of the
plains. The grass becomes thinner here as it rises up the slope of the small
hill to the east.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
You are now deep within the plains. Totally surrounded by them, you feel
somewhat humbled by nature, and it's power. The grass is light, and easy to pass
through, and is cool and moist to the touch. In the distance you can see
mountains rising up above the horizon.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
Grassy plains surround you as you make your way through them, pushing aside the
grass when it becomes too tall to walk over. A few trees grow in the plains
here, and to the south you can see a great wall of trees. To the west the plains
rise up to some small hills.
name: Loudwater Plains

desc:
The path here continues on, as paths tend to do. To the east the wild forest
continues, and to the west is a small hill. The path continues to head in a
north-south direction.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
The road goes onwards and onwards, seeming to last forever. To the east is a
large, wild forest, where the trees are growing so close together that it is
hard to see much past the first tree.
name: Path to the Mountains

desc:
An old, overgrown path takes you north from here around a large forest extending
to the east. Looking south, you see the path leading up a hill to a bridge over
the river. You remember old tales of a large trade-route, leading between the
dwarves in Erebor, the Lonely Mountain, and Imladris, the home of the proud
Noldor.
name: Path to the High Pass

desc:
You are standing on a path leading up a slope to the south. It turns north and
east here. To the east, you see the northern end of an old bridge leading across
the river Bruinen. You turn your eyes to the north-east and see a large forest
spreading across the lands.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
This is high up on a cliff above the river. South of here, a stone bridge can
take you across the river to the path that continues to the south. You follow
the path to the west, leading you down from the cliff and then further north,
eventually to the High Pass through the Misty Mountains. You can also enter a
wild forest to the north.
name: North of the Bridge

desc:
As you push your way through the scratchy pine needles, the forest gets thicker
and thicker. Soon movement will not be possible any more. To the east and west,
the trees are so close together that movement is not possible.
name: Wild Forest

desc:
As you struggle through the trees, earning a few scratches here and there, you
realise that the trees here are just not worth fighting. The only direction that
movement is possible is back to the north, from where you came.
name: Wild Forest

desc:
The forest that surrounds you brings you peace, but you can sense some other
feeling underlying the peace, subverting it. The forest here is very thick, and
the only direction that movement is possible is back to the east.
name: Wild Forest

desc:
This is the edge of a wild forest. It extends to the north, and you can hear the
howling of rare beasts. Dare you really enter this old forest, forgot by all men
a long time ago? You can get back out to the east, from where you also hear the
splashing water of the south fork of the Bruinen.
name: Wild Forest

desc:
This bridge is considerably high above the River Bruinen which lies below. The
stone arch carries that carries travellers safely across, stretches the width of
the river, which continues upstream into the Misty Mountains. Downstream, is the
northern bank. Beyond that, barely noticeable in the distance, is a large house,
perhaps it is Imladris, the home of Elrond. The bridge bears the mark of the
dwarves on a stone plaque that is fastened to its middle.
name: Bridge over the Bruinen

desc:
You are on a high cliff at the Bruinen. A small path leads north, onto a bridge
over to the north side of the river. You can go down a slope to the west,
leading down to the riverside. The path continues to the east, and beyond it a
plateau stretches east. When you raise your eyes, you see the grey mountains in
the distance, and you wonder what foul creatures it hides.
name: South of the Bridge

desc:
The light is glittering in the surface beautifully as you slowly drift down the
river. If it only could be like this forever... To the west, the river speeds up
as it whirls around some rocks in the river.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
Here, the river is flowing faster among some rocks in the river, carrying you
downstream, eventually to reach the city that once was the capital of Cardolan -
Tharbad. If you hurry, you might get ashore on the north bank, where a grassy
field stretches north. The southern bank is full of scattered rocks. To the west
the river flows under the pine forest on the northern bank, and is cloaked in
shadow.
name: The South Fork of the Bruinen

desc:
The stream turns westward, pressed between two rocky walls, and begins to hasten
as it draws near to a great waterfall. Its mighty roar reverberates between the
walls, and although a rising cloud of mist veils the horizon behind the
waterfall you can guess the valley widens further downstream. At the edge of the
cliffs a line of firs stands out against the sky.
name: Nearing a Waterfall

desc:
Swept out into midair by the waterfall, you experience a moment of
weightlessness while you plunge downwards at increasing speed. It doesn't last
long though, as you soon reach the bottom of the gorge. The last thing you
notice is the rocks just below the surface of the water, against which your
bones are smashed to pieces.
name: Roaring Waterfall

desc:
You are standing in a grassy field just north of the southern fork of the great
river Bruinen. Looking south, across the river, you see a rock-covered area
before the large wilderness in the south. Just east of here, a path leads up a
slope to the northern end of a bridge, high above the river.
name: In the Grass

desc:
Looking down, you see nothing but green grass covering your feet. To the east,
just in front of a large forest, a path leads north from a bridge over the
Bruinen. The river itself with its splashing and sparkling water is a bit
further south of here.
name: In the Grass

desc:
As you first enter the dank cave, a musty odour comes from the back. Around the
room are a number of bones from small animals. There are a few worn spots and
some tufts of fur here and there, making the room look very lived in.
name: Cave inside a Hill

desc:
The edge of a fir forest peeks over the edge of a sheer cliff to the west, with
one particularly huge tree spreading its branches well out over the edge. A
small path follows the feet of the cliff wall, leading northwards to where the
Bruinen flows over a shallow pebbled ford. To the south it sinks in a wooded
V-shaped dell.
name: A Rough Trail

desc:
A forest of silvery firs spreads westward from the bottom of a V-shaped dell,
while to the south a small footpath winds among the dense underbrush. To the
east the ground rises more steeply, and prunes and brambles grow so thick and
matted that there is no way to pass them. To get to the top of the slope, where
the forest extends again, you would have to make a long detour.
name: A Wooded Dell of Silvery Firs

desc:
This section of the trail skirts around a deep dike to the south, where an
ivy-clad wall bars the gap between two hills. It was built during the long siege
by the Witch-king, as part of the ancient fortifications whose ruins still loom
on the crest of the hills. A forest of silvery firs spread to the north.
name: Path to the Misty Mountains

desc:
